name: Commander Type
description: You've got a special neural interface for the manipulation of traps on the battlefield and the management of Gears directly under your command.
effect: At the beginning of your Turn you may spend 1 Energy to use a single Support Upgrade you own without having to spend an Action.

name: Defensive Technician
description: Techniques are unusual, difficult attacks that leave you open to counterattacks. Your Gear is programmed with defensive maneuvers to turn their eccentric maneuvering into a form of defense as well as offense.
effect: When you use a Technique, you may spend 2 Energy to increase your Defense by 5 against the target of the attack until the beginning of your next Turn. If you used Zweihander, you don't grant the target extra Advantages to attacking you until the beginning of your next Turn.

name: Early Warning and Control
description: The EWAC system links your Gear to the rest of your team, feeding you information on everything they can detect. An onboard AI processes and relays this information to give your squadmates battlefield support and improve their performance.
effect: When you use a Support Upgrade with a Cost of 5 you may spend 4 Energy to restore said Upgrade after use.

name: Expert Support
description: Your Gear is equipped with the cutting edge in targeting systems, guaranteeing that you will almost never incur friendly fire.
effect: You may always Attack Enemies in the middle of a Duel without running the risk of hitting your Ally as long as it is with a Weapon that hits single targets. When using any area of effect Weapon or Support Upgrade, you may choose any Zones within their area of influence to be safe from the attack and from any secondary effects the Weapon may carry.

name: Master Technician
description: Your Gear has combat subroutines installed in it to facilitate pulling off tricky combat maneuvers more than once. This way you can repeat supermoves in a sequence of cinematic explosions.
effect: When you use a Technique, you may spend 2 Energy to restore it after use, treating it as if its one-time Tension bonus hadn't been spent. If you used Missile Massacre, you don't suffer Disadvantages to future attacks against the target.

name: Portal Generator
description: This device uses the gravitational properties of G-Reactors through a specially crafted Synchronizer. It opens a rift in spacetime, creating a stable tunnel between separate points—what is colloquially called a "Portal".
effect: At the beginning of your Turn you may spend any amount of Energy to create two portals connecting separate Zones. One of the portals appears in your Zone while the other appears within a Range from your Zone up to the Energy spent this way. Units that stand on top of either Zone can treat the other Zone as if it was adjacent to theirs. These portals last until the beginning of your next Turn.

name: Power Conversion
description: Through tampering with circuitry that many would never dare touch, you know how to repurpose power from your Gear's offensive systems to other mechanisms.
effect: At the beginning of your Turn you may increase your available Energy by an amount equal to the current Tension until the beginning of your next Turn but you do not gain the benefit of Tension to Offensive Actions this Round either.

name: Slippery Chassis
description: Your Gear's frame type has superior mobility and is small enough to make stealth possible as a giant robot. This makes you ideal candidate for reconnaissance duty and covert operations in general.
effect: You don't need to Disengage to move away from a Duel, and don't take Damage when you Move after being Suppressed.

name: Stealth Field
description: This Synchronizer modification allows the G-Reactor to surround the Gear with an undetectable field, invisible to everything outside of the naked senses. Maintaining this field takes a lot of effort and energy from the user, but that is the price of being invisible to all known technology.
effect: When Maneuvering any Might Tests against you suffer two Disadvantages unless the attack is made with Weapons that have the Blast, Burst or Line abilities.

name: Versatile Model
description: Your Gear is built to take advantage of balanced loadouts, mixing close quarters and ranged attacks. It switches between weapons and attack routines much faster than normal, catching enemies by surprise.
effect: You gain an additional Advantage to Might Tests using Melee Weapons if your last Might Test was made with a Shooting Weapon and viceversa.

name: Artillery Frame
description: You pack bigger weapons than everyone else. Beams are wider, missiles make bigger explosions, and you chew through magazines like candy.
effect: All your Blast, Burst and Line Weapons gain an Advantage to their Might Tests and you increase the radius of your Blasts by 1 additional Zone. This includes your Blast effects from Powers or Upgrades.

name: Assistant
description: You get a second pair of hands to help you out in the battlefield in the form of a NPC subpilot. The know-how and support from this subpilot will improve your Gear's efficiency beyond what you could manage on your own.
effect: You gain a Subpilot, who grants you any one Genre Power from the available lists to your character and another Genre Point to use during Operations.

name: Duelist Model
description: Your Gear was engineered to corner foe and force them to face you, punishing all that dare turn their backs on you with extreme prejudice.
effect: When an Enemy uses the Disengage Action to get away from you, you get a free Offensive Action using a Melee Weapon against them. Your Melee Weapons gain an Advantage to Might Tests.

name: Extreme Body
description: Close combat is a terrible idea against your Gear. This could be due to an electrified armor system, offensive use of a forcefield or because it's a biomechanical monster that spews acid everywhere but especially when wounded.
effect: At the beginning of your Turn you may spend 1 Energy to have all Zones occupied by your body count as Extreme Terrain for Enemies in them until the beginning of your next Turn. Abilities that can usually ignore the effects of Terrain will not help against this.

name: Integrated Weapons
description: Your Weapons are integrated to the Gear's frame. Cannons are mounted to the shoulders, blades attached to the hands and knees. You are now effectively immune to disarming.
effect: You may use your Weapons even if the Area they were allocated to has been Maimed.

name: Maneuverable Transformer
description: Your transforming robot is built to maximize the advantages of constant form changes. The shifts keep throwing enemies off; even when expecting them, your moves are too fast for most pilots to keep up with.
effect: Whenever you Transform you may choose to either have all Enemies suffer a Disadvantage to all Might Tests against you or to gain an Advantage to your Might Tests. Either benefit lasts until the beginning of your next Turn.

name: Nanopaste Skeleton
description: Your Gear has an in-built dispenser of nanomachine paste used for repairing its internals. It has a slow recharge time between uses while the dispenser produces more nanobots, but it's as efficient as it gets otherwise.
effect: At the beginning of your Turn you may ignore the effects of Maiming until the beginning of your next Turn. If you do, you can't use this ability again during your next Turn.

name: Pulling Field
description: You know how to use the G-Reactor's gravitational bending to subtly draw enemies away from your allies and towards you instead.
effect: At the beginning of your Turn you may choose one Enemy within 1 Zone and inflict on them a Disadvantage to all Might Tests that do not target you until the beginning of your next Turn. If you attack the chosen Enemy during your Turn, you inflict two Disadvantages instead. This effect persists even if the Enemy moves away from you.

name: Skirmisher Frame
description: Your Gear is built for evasive maneuvers while sustaining acceleration, avoiding obstacles or projectiles on the move.
effect: You gain an Advantage to all Initiative Tests. If you Move at least 5 Zones with your Action during one of your Turns, all Enemies suffer a Disadvantage to all Might Tests against you until the beginning of your next Turn.

name: Sniper Model
description: Equipped with very expensive and sensitive targeting systems, your Gear could hit a target beyond the horizon with enough force for strategic bombing of key targets.
effect: Increase the Maximum Range of your Long Range Shooting Weapons by twice your Systems instead of just your Systems. All your Shooting Weapons gain an Advantage to Might Tests against targets at Range 1 or higher.

name: Experimental Reactor
description: A specially designed G-Reactor with greater energy output, with more efficient weapon-feeding capacity and greater destructive power of all energy weapons.
effect: Your Beam Weapons have their base Energy cost (but not the Boost cost) reduced to 0 and gain an Advantage to all Might Tests.

name: Guardian of Steel
description: Your Gear is enhanced and optimized to protect your team. This can be in the form of large clouds of chaff, projecting multiple holograms on the battlefield or extending a massive forcefield over nearby allies.
effect: When Maneuvering you may spend 5 Energy to share the Defense bonus with any Allies within 1 Zone (they lose the bonus outside of that Range). You do not have to give up your benefits from Maneuvering to share them this way.

name: Internal Fortification
description: The internal systems of your Gear are segmented and fortified to protect against massive damage. It doesn't matter how much one of your areas gets hit, the damage to the rest of the body will be minimized.
effect: Whenever you lose a Level of Threshold, all damage carrying over from the same instance of Damage to the next Threshold Level is halved.

name: Invincible Alloy
description: Your giant robot dons an experimental new composite alloy reinforced to withstand the rigors of battle better than most others. Even your internals are reinforced, making your equipment that much harder to disable.
effect: At the beginning of your Turn you may spend 2 Energy to ignore the effects of Maiming until the beginning of your next Turn. The first time that you would be destroyed during an Operation, you remain functional with a Threshold of 1 instead. This works against any source of Damage except Techniques.

name: Limit Engine
description: The Synchronizer can draw energy from the user in a pinch, and a specially designed cockpit permits their pilots to transform anger, courage and the will to live into energy. The downside of this 'Spiral Drive' is that the system encourages pilots to fight while at the brink of defeat, but in a desperate enough circumstance that can be considered an upside.
effect: For each Level of Threshold you are missing you treat Tension as if it was one point higher. This modifier to Tension applies to everything you use Tension for, even Extreme Terrain.

name: Superior Integration
description: Not only are all your weapons built into your unit's frame but they also have dedicated capacitors that overcharge them with extra power on first use.
effect: You may use your Weapons even if the Area they were allocated to has been Maimed. The first time you use each of your Weapons during an Operation, it gains an additional Advantage to its Might Test.

name: Superior Morphing
description: Your robot's internal framework has been modified to allow for more versatile changes between equipment. Your jets can turn into cannons and your wheels into shields. The faster transformations are a nice bonus.
effect: You may switch between equipment Frames at the beginning of your Turn instead of using an Action, and may Transform for 1 Energy instead of 2. When you purchase this Upgrade, make two sets of Features, External Upgrades or Weapons with a total cost of 10 MP for each set. Assign each set to a different Form, but all in the same Areas. The Cost in MP of these sets is already paid by this Upgrade.

name: The Beast
description: Your mecha is a Familiar, a Mechabeast that has been tamed and bonded to you through shared symbiotic cells. Your model in particular eschews most weaponry, preferring to fight using natural weapons instead, and is at their fiercest when cornered.
effect: Your Default Weapons don't suffer an innate Disadvantage to their use and gain an Advantage to Might Tests for each Level of Threshold you are missing. After attacking with a Default Weapon, you might spend 2 Energy to make your attack immune to the effects of Active Defenses.

name: Warp Step
description: Some Familiars are capable of instant transmission between locations in the blink of an eye. The best at Steel Dragon Industries are still trying to figure out how to reproduce and replicate this technology for use with more traditional Gear types.
effect: You may ignore Enemies and Terrain (even Impassable Terrain) along the way whenever you Move with your Actions. This lets you escape from Duels without having to Disengage.

name: Transpatial Randomizer
description: This handgun-shaped device might just be the most bizarre use of G-Reactors yet. It uses the Synchronizer to transform the pilot's latent psychic energy into unpredictable but largely beneficial results with each pull of the trigger.
effect: At the beginning of your Turn you may spend 1 Energy to choose a Support Upgrade at random. You get to use said Upgrade at no Action cost. Each subsequent use of this Upgrade costs an additional point of Energy. This does not spend said Upgrades and you may use Upgrades that you do not own this way.
conditions: Roll 1d100 to determine which Support Upgrade is used (two ten-sided dice, one as tens and one as units): 01-07 Assisted Targeting, 08-14 Dividing Field, 15-21 Overcharge, 22-28 Remote Hotfix, 29-35 Support Fire, 36-42 Supply Delivery, 43-49 Surprise Minefield, 50-56 Targeting Disruption, 57-61 Airstrike, 62-66 Blue Screen Virus, 67-71 Cryogenic Blast, 72-76 Electromagnetic Detonator, 77-81 Ensnaring Trap, 82-86 Fire at Will, 87-91 Gravity Manipulators, 92-96 Jamming Barrage, 97-00 Any Support Upgrade of your choice.

name: Absorbing Armor
description: Gears can be built out of a variety of composite alloys designed to prevent against specific forms of attack or coated with special particles that do the same. A neat side effect of this technology is that they forgo the need to paint your giant robot, making even dull gray metal look fabulous.
effect: Choose one of Melee, Shooting, Beam or non-Beam when you take this Upgrade. In response to the results of an Enemy Might Test against you using a Weapon of the chosen type, you may spend 1 Energy to increase your Defense by 3 against it, and if that is enough to block all the Damage you would have taken from the Might Test it costs no Energy instead.

name: Custom Defense
description: You can equip a variety of defensive mechanisms to protect yourself from enemy attacks at the cost of energy. Examples include portable shields for close quarters attacks, nanomachine bubbles that slow down high velocity projectiles and a variety of chaff dispensers or jamming devices.
effect: Choose one of Melee, Shooting, Beam or non-Beam when you take this Upgrade. In response to the results of an Enemy Might Test against you using a Weapon of the chosen type, you may spend 1 Energy to increase your Defense by 3 against it, or 2 Energy to increase your Defense by 5 against it instead.

name: Learning Computer
description: This self-learning AI compensates for the wear and tear of battle by looking for openings in the attack patterns of the enemy and moving the Gear on its own.
effect: In response to the results of an Enemy Might Test against you, you may spend 2 Energy to increase your Defense by the current Tension against it. This does not count Tension bonuses to the Offensive Action.

name: Reactive Booster
description: Reactive Boosters read specific patterns and automatically move the Gear in response to even the fastest of attacks. Custom variants include CQC footwork and anti-missile cartwheels, among others.
effect: In response to the results of an Enemy Might Test against you, you may spend 1 Energy to increase your Defense by an amount equal to half your Speed against it.

name: Reflecting Barrier
description: This highly specialized defense system sends bullets, missiles and even particle beams astray. A minuscule portion of which are even sent back at their user.
effect: In response to the results of an Enemy Might Test against you using a Shooting Weapon, you may spend 1 Energy to increase your Defense by 3 against it, or 2 Energy to also reflect 3 points of Damage back to the attacker. This Damage is dealt after the rest of the attack resolves.

name: Repulsion Field
description: A forcefield that uses gravity manipulation technology to slow down incoming projectiles and enemies in melee. At maximum output it can even push close enemies backwards to keep them away from you.
effect: In response to the results of an Enemy Might Test against you using a Melee Weapon, you may spend 1 Energy to increase your Defense by 3 against it, or 2 Energy to also push the target 1 Zone away from you in a direction of your choice. This push happens after the rest of the attack resolves. Repulsion Field cannot push Enemies into Zones with other Units or into Impassable Terrain.

name: Absolute Barrier
description: The most common all-round defensive application of the G-Reactor's gravity manipulation capabilities is the Absolute Barrier. Most other forcefields are specialized variants of it. The Absolute Barrier repels things through a constant series of violent explosions. It blocks nearly everything you can throw at it this way, while glowing like it is on fire.
effect: At the beginning of your Turn you may spend any amount of Energy to create a shield that blocks Damage until next Round. The amount of Damage it can block is equal to 150% the amount of Energy spent, so 1 point of Energy blocks 1 Damage but 2 points of Energy block 3 Damage. An active Absolute Barrier means you can't use other Active Defenses.

name: G-Charger
description: You carry a multitude of batteries that can be activated to boost your Gear with a surge of short-lived power.
effect: As a Utility Action, you may increase your available Energy by 5 until your next Turn.

name: Reload
description: You've got magazines attached to your arms, backup drones in your backpack, and more extras of everything you use that you could possibly need.
effect: As a Utility Action, you may restock a single One Shot Weapon or Support Upgrade of yours that has been spent.

name: Jury Rig
description: You carry a dispenser of fast-repair nanomachines and enough metaphorical (or literal) duct tape to keep allies from falling apart.
effect: As a Utility Action, you may spend any amount of Energy to restore that much Threshold to yourself or to any one Ally within 5 Zones.

name: Regenerative
description: The Familiar brand of mechs, those made from Mechabeasts, often have astounding regenerative properties to fix their vital areas. These are greatly supplemented by nanoskin alloy systems that also fix their armored shells.
effect: At the beginning of your Turn you may spend any amount of Energy to restore that much Threshold to yourself. This Upgrade uses two of your available Restorations per Operation instead of just one.

name: Resupply
description: You have a comically big backpack with all the supplies you and your team could ever need to keep their weapons functional. It does not slow you down much, but it does stick out like a sore thumb so be careful to not letting it get caught in the crossfire.
effect: As a Utility Action, you may restock any number of One Shot Weapons or Support Upgrades that have been spent, spending that many of your available Restorations per Operation instead of just one. You may use Resupply on yourself or an Ally within 5 Zones, but all Weapons and Upgrades must belong to the same Unit.

name: Maritime Module
description: Your Gear is designed to thrive in aquatic environments where others can't even move properly. It can even withstand deep sea pressure.
effect: At the beginning of your Turn, you may spend 1 Energy to gain the following benefits until the beginning of your next Turn: You may ignore the effects of Difficult and Withering Terrain. You may halve all Damage you would take from Extreme Terrain. This Upgrade only works in water Terrain.

name: Zero-G Module
description: Your unit is outfitted with multiple systems to improve its performance in space. It comes with two sets of rotating vernier thrusters to facilitate maneuvering in a vacuum and its G-Reactor produces a shield to protect the Gear from space radiation.
effect: At the beginning of your Turn, you may spend 1 Energy to gain the following benefits until the beginning of your next Turn: You may ignore the effects of Difficult and Withering Terrain. You may halve all Damage you would take from Extreme Terrain. This Upgrade only works in space Terrain.

name: Anti-Gravity
description: This system grants the Gear freedom of movement in three dimensions. Its only complication is that it doesn't allow the unit to go higher than a few hundred meters even in optimal conditions. This means Gears cannot fly over mountains.
effect: At the beginning of your Turn, you may spend 2 Energy to Fly until the beginning of your next Turn. Flying has the following effects: You may ignore the effects of Difficult, Defensive, Extreme, Impassable and Withering Terrain (unless they're Anti-Air Terrain variants). You may shoot through a Zone occupied by an Enemy to reach another behind it as if they weren't there. Enemies may also ignore any cover that you would provide for your Allies or that your Allies and Impassable Terrain would provide for you. You cannot benefit from Defensive Terrain (unless it is the Anti-Air Terrain variant). This Upgrade has no effect in water or in space Terrain.

name: Overbooster
description: Originally thought up as a way to help with space exploration and colonization, the Overbooster has been repurposed to provide Gears with frankly excessive bursts of speed in any environment.
effect: At the beginning of your Turn, you may spend 1 Energy to Move up to 3 extra Zones with your next Action this Round, or 2 Energy to Move up to 5 extra Zones instead.

name: Reversible Thrusters
description: These let you move at full speed to get away from the enemy without having to turn your back on them.
effect: Anytime you take an Offensive Action, you may Move backwards during it. The target must remain within a valid range after moving.

name: Assisted Targeting
description: With assistance from your allies using advanced computer targeting systems you and your allies can mark and track targets more efficiently than if you were to do it manually.
effect: You or an Ally gain the benefits of the Aim Action to their next Offensive Action this Round.

name: Dividing Field
description: A system that bends space to create a circular battlefield around the user. Land, air and water are simultaneously frozen and compressed around this Dividing Field until it recedes, leaving everything exactly as it used to be.
effect: All Zones within Range turn into Plain Terrain, losing all other Terrain types, until the beginning of your next Turn. This includes Impassable Terrain. Afterwards those Zones return to normal.

name: Overcharge
description: The Overcharger looks like a lightning gun that would make Tesla proud, but actually shoots a stream of nanobots carrying a multitude of Gravagnium crystals. These crystals will essentially overclock a Gear temporarily, making it run at 200% power for a very brief period of time.
effect: Increase the Energy available to yourself or an Ally by 4 during their next Turn. Multiple Overcharges on the same Unit do not stack.

name: Remote Hotfix
description: Through redirecting power and manual overrides, any and all sorts of malfunctions can be fixed. You can reoptimize systems on the spot, overcoming unforeseen difficulties as if you had been ready for them all along.
effect: You or an Ally ignore the effects of Maiming until the beginning of your next Turn.

name: Support Fire
description: Your sidekicks aren't all that great at shooting down enemies on their own. But they are pretty good at providing cover fire and flanking the foes you're already targeting.
effect: You can grant the Crippling ability to one of your Weapons or that of an ally for their next Offensive Action.

name: Supply Delivery
description: You can request supplies in the middle of a battle. Planes can drop crates full of ammo, VTOLs deliver replacement guns for you to pick up, and sometimes cannons just plain shoot spare parts in your general direction that you must catch mid-flight yourself.
effect: You or an Ally restock a single One Shot Weapon that has been spent during this Operation.

name: Surprise Minefield
description: Your enemies always fall for the same trick. They confidently stride forward, advancing towards you thinking that the battlefield is in their control already, until the ground under them suddenly blows them up.
effect: A single Zone turns into Extreme Terrain until the beginning of your next Turn.

name: Targeting Disruption
description: You hack into an enemy's targeting systems and make it harder for them to use their weapons systems. However, your direct connection to them leaves you vulnerable.
effect: Choose one Enemy to inflict them two Disadvantages to all Might Tests that do not target you until the beginning of your next Turn.

name: Airstrike
description: A heavy bombardment run on a designated area with a specially prepared payload. There is just so much firepower spread out that forcefields are useless against it.
effect: All Units within a target area the size of a Blast (3) suffer an amount of Damage equal to half your Systems.

name: Blue Screen Virus
description: You spread the fear and terror of operating system failure among your enemies with a virus that forces them to reboot, wasting precious time.
effect: One Grunt loses their Action during their next Turn.

name: Cryogenic Blast
description: You deploy a cloud of freezing gas over the targets. It is not going to completely disable giant robots, but it will slow joints down and cause multiple internal malfunctions.
effect: All Zones within a target area the size of a Blast (3) turn into Difficult and Withering Terrain until the beginning of your next Turn.

name: Electromagnetic Detonator
description: Gears tend to be shielded enough from electromagnetic pulses to not be disabled by those. However, they are not entirely immune, a well-placed EMP can still provide a critical opening to defeat them with.
effect: One Enemy has their available Energy halved and will suffer 2 points of Damage per Energy point they spend until the beginning of your next Turn.

name: Ensnaring Trap
description: This entrapment system, thinly disguised as part of the background, webs down foes with reinforced wires.
effect: One Enemy has their Guard and Speed halved until the beginning of your next Turn. If they are using Anti-Gravity or another ability that grants them flight, it stops working for that duration as well.

name: Fire at Will
description: You have allies behind the front lines ready to provide artillery support. They are not a match versus Gears or Mechabeasts on their own, but their firepower can turn the tide of a battle.
effect: One Enemy Tests Speed against a DN of 10 plus your Systems and if they fail suffer the amount that they missed the DN by as Damage.

name: Gravity Manipulators
description: You have deployed gravity manipulators along the battlefield and can use them to launch allies and enemies alike in any direction you please.
effect: Slide any Unit a number of Zones up to half your Systems. Should the target be sent in the direction of another Unit, you may have them both crash and suffer an amount of Damage equal to the distance moved this way, then the movement from this Upgrade halts. This can be used to break up Duels, even ones you are in.

name: Jamming Barrage
description: A series of missiles that explode into a short lived cloud of particles that confuse the targeting systems of all enemies in the vicinity, causing them to be unable to distinguish friend from foe temporarily.
effect: All Units within a target area the size of a Blast (3) have their Might halved until the beginning of your next Turn.

name: Expansion Pack
description: An Expansion Pack effectively provides an extra limb, and should you ever be pushed to the limit, you can even eject it in the way of an incoming attack to soften the blow. They come in many forms, from a backpack with a bunch of weapons attached to a comically big belly that hides precious equipment within the safety of all that armor.
effect: An Expansion Pack is a separate Area that can hold up to 30 MP in External Upgrades or Weapons and it cannot be Maimed by Enemy attacks. Whenever you would be destroyed during an Operation, you can purge your Expansion Pack and remain functional with a Threshold of 1 instead. Doing so will render the Pack and its contents unusable for the rest of the Operation. Purging works against any source of Damage except Techniques. You still must purchase those Upgrades and Weapons with MP.

name: Secret Equipment
description: Your Gear has a very advanced AI that is something of a black box, to the point that it seemingly has a will of its own. A good portion of its equipment is hidden and locked away from use until the AI determines the battle has gone for long enough and needs to end now.
effect: When you purchase this Upgrade make a set of External Upgrades or Weapons worth 30 MP in total. You can use these Upgrades and Weapons only when Tension is of 5 or higher, but they cannot be Maimed by Enemy attacks. The Cost in MP of the set is already paid by this Upgrade.

name: Frame
description: You have modular equipment that you can switch around between missions to sport a stronger or sleeker build when it is necessary to do so.
effect: Choose two of Might, Guard, Systems or Speed when you take this Upgrade. You switch the value of the chosen Attributes around when you Equip this Frame. You may equip or unequip this Frame before deployment or as an Action while Docked inside your Base.

name: Transformation
description: Your Gear is a transformer, going from flying robot to a faster plane form or a sturdier tank form.
effect: Choose two of Might, Guard, Systems or Speed when you take this Upgrade. You switch the value of the chosen Attributes around when you Transform. You may switch back and forth from this Transformation at the beginning of your Turn by spending 2 Energy or as an Action.

name: Super Combination
description: Some Gears are designed from the start to combine into something greater than the sum of their parts, with each component of the body having assigned its own pilot. Their teamwork creates a giant robot an order of magnitude more powerful than regular ones.
effect: All components must choose one of them to be the Lead Unit when they take this Upgrade. All Subpilots then choose one of the four External Areas belonging to the Lead. Combining has the following effects: The lead Unit gains all the Upgrades and Weapons of the other components, assigning the External ones to their chosen Area. The Attributes of the Combined Unit are the averaged values of all the components together. When an External Area of the combined Unit is Maimed, the Lead Unit loses Upgrades and Weapons held there as normal but it does not lose a Subpilot or recalculate its Attributes.

name: Unison Combination
description: These mechs are designed from the start to crash together in an orgy of steel and speed lines. Once formed, this combiner can transform into multiple forms as the situation needs it, allowing every participant a chance at being the one in control according to their skills.
effect: You and your Allies may now Combine. Combining has the following effects: The new Unit has a number of forms equal to its number of component Mecha, with each one having a different component as the Lead Unit. Each of these forms has the External Upgrades, Weapons, Might, Guard, Systems and Speed of the current Lead. Its Internal Upgrades, Threshold and Energy are shared, using the average values of all the components together. All Pilots of a Unison Combiner may freely share their Genre Points with each other and do not need to spend Actions to participate in Synchro Attacks with each other.

name: Universal Component
description: Your mecha is a support unit designed to power up other mechs by attaching to them as a backpack, extra head, or even transforming into a giant multipurpose weapon for them to wield.
effect: You can now Combine with any of your Allies. Combining has the following effects: You become a Subpilot for the lead Unit. The combined Unit gains all of your Upgrades and Weapons, assigning the External ones to their corresponding Areas. The Attributes of the Combined Unit are the averaged values of both components together, plus 1 to Might, Guard, Systems and Speed.
conditions: If you combine in the middle of an Operation, anything you've spent is restored. This means Restoration Upgrades, One Shot and Technique Weapons are treated as if they had not been used at all.

name: Invincible Super Combination
description: Because the Super Combination was clearly not enough, all of the component Mecha have been powered to form the ultimate invincible superhuman warrior of love and courage. Make sure to give the combiner a name that references its number of pilots, such as Sunbot 3 or Voltace V.
effect: All Units are restored to full after Combination. This means Threshold, Energy, Restoration Upgrades, One Shot and Technique Weapons are treated as if they had not been used at all. The Attributes of the Combined Unit are the averaged values of all the components together, plus 1 to Might, Guard, Systems and Speed per Subpilot.
conditions: Requires Super Combination; all components must take it at the same time. MP cost scales by Power Level: PL 0: 20, PL 1: 30, PL 2: 40, PL 3: 50, PL 4: 60, PL 5: 70. When you gain a new Power Level, you go into an MP deficit and must spend your first 10 MP to pay that debt.

name: Base Unit
description: Your unit is the carrier that your entire allied formation revolves around. That makes you the primary target to any and all enemies, so do try to be careful.
effect: Any allied Unit within Move Distance may choose to Dock inside you as an Action. They may redeploy outside with another Action, launching a single Zone away from you. If you are ever Defeated, so is your Squad. Base Units cannot Engage in or be Engaged into a duel.

name: Extreme Fortification
description: You are reinforced internally and externally, at the price of having to use smaller and less powerful energy reactors. Through this method, you can survive in the most inhospitable places known to mankind and weather some pretty heavy attacks as a bonus. Just watch your energy reserves.
effect: You halve all Damage you would usually take from any source that isn't a Might Test, but your available Energy is halved. This also works for abilities that are a secondary effect of using a Weapon but separate from the Might Test itself.

name: Flyer
description: You are capable of atmospheric flight, but it is the only way you can get around. This can be used to represent any method of flight as long as it is the only way the machine is capable of moving.
effect: You always move by Flying. Flight has the following effects: You may ignore the effects of Difficult, Defensive, Extreme, Impassable and Withering Terrain (unless they're Anti-Air Terrain variants). You may shoot through a Zone occupied by an Enemy to reach another behind it as if they weren't there. Enemies may also ignore any cover that you would provide for your Allies or that your Allies and Impassable Terrain would provide for you. You cannot benefit from Defensive Terrain (unless it is the Anti-Air Terrain variant). You may not Move as part of your Actions while in water or after being forced to land.

name: Oldtype
description: You're hardier and more stubborn than most, much like your Gear. The downside is that you're stuck in your ways, remaining incapable of adapting to unexpected circumstances in battle.
effect: You cannot use Default or Alternative Genre Powers other than Live Another Day and Parting Shot. Whenever you lose a Level of Threshold, all damage carrying over from the same instance of Damage to the next Threshold Level is halved.

name: Omnienvironmental
description: Your mecha was made not to do battle but for scouting operations and is adapted to all sorts of environments. It is not shielded for extreme conditions, but the added maneuverability is very helpful there.
effect: Ignore all negative Terrain conditions except Extreme and Impassable Terrain, but only have half your Energy Attribute to spend every Round.

name: Power Suit
description: Instead of a Gear of your own you ride a more powerful Exosuit into battle. It is somehow tough enough to stand up to the big kids of the playground, but the smaller size has a disadvantage in that direct hits hurt that much more.
effect: This Unit does not lose any abilities allocated to its Areas from Maiming. Instead each Area lost to damage halves one of your Attributes. Losing the Head halves your Systems, the Torso halves your Guard, the Arms halve your Might and the Legs your Speed.

name: Remora Frame
description: A different take on how to build a component unit. This small Gear instead uses others as carriers and shields.
effect: You may Dock within a willing Ally as if they were a Base Unit. You cannot Dock into another Remora Frame. Halve all your own Movement, Upgrade and Weapon Ranges. Maximum Weapon Ranges are rounded up instead of down.

name: Sibling Model
description: Your unit is part of a series, designed with combination attacks in mind. The movements of individual models flow smoothly into those of its sibling Gears... As long as every single one of them is in sync. Otherwise it is just a mess.
effect: Synchro Attacks increase the result of your Might Test by 5 for each participating Ally instead of gaining two Advantages. You can only Synchro Attack with other Sibling Models.

name: Terrain Specialist
description: A lot of machines derived from the technology that is used for Gears are not humanoid. You can find anything from mecha-crabs for aquatic environments to spidermechs specialized in ground combat.
effect: Choose one of water, space, or land when you take this Feature. While you are in said environment you gain the benefit of Defensive Terrain, ignore the effects of Difficult Terrain, and may shoot through a Zone occupied by an Enemy to reach another behind it as if they weren't there. Other environments count as Difficult Terrain for you.

name: Unstable Reactor
description: Your Gear runs on something that R&D should have known better than to try. Your power output is unparalleled, but you know any mistake you make could be your last.
effect: Increase your available Energy during your Turns by 1. When you lose your fourth Level of Threshold you cause a massive explosion dealing your Threshold Attribute, your available Energy, and the current Tension to everything within 5 Zones. The blast includes your ejected Core and this Damage cannot be prevented, not even Live Another Day.
